summary: Adopt two build practices from YanWenKun/ComfyUI-Docker: (1) RUN --quick-test-for-ci --cpu after the node bake so an import failure fails the BUILD (the MPI-149 kornia/LTXVideo class that shipped and died live as Node Stage1_Bypass not found; also automates the hand-run '8 nodes register' gate from MPI-139); (2) pip constraints file pinning the cu130 trio so torch drift is unresolvable, with the MPI-244 assert kept as the belt. Blocked on MPI-340 - build to the dev tag, never rebuild released v0.16.0.

todo -> doing. Dockerfile guards landed in mpi-ci/cubric-vision-pod/Dockerfile: (1) node-import smoke test after the MPI-244 assert - runs main.py --quick-test-for-ci --cpu to a log file and GREPS for IMPORT FAILED, because the flag does a plain exit(0) and custom-node import failures are only logged (verified in upstream main.py at v0.27.0 and v0.28.0); the brief's bare RUN would have been vacuously green. (2) constraints file generated from pip list right after the cu130 trio install + ENV PIP_CONSTRAINT=/opt/constraints.txt, so the node bake, the kornia pin and the wrapper's runtime installs cannot resolve a non-cu130 torch; MPI-244 assert kept. Docs section added to docs/builder/02-image-and-rebuild.md. NOT yet built - the dev-tag CI dispatch rides with MPI-342, and the unpin-kornia proof that the smoke test bites is still outstanding.
